title: Private Boat Hire in Marbella: Costs, Tips & Best Boats for 2026
html:
Private boat hire in Marbella offers a sophisticated way to explore the Costa del Sol, with departures from Puerto Banús and prices typically starting from €350 for a half-day on a motorboat. BoatHire24 connects you directly with local owners, giving you access to a range of craft without the hassle of rental desks.

Where to set off in Marbella?

Puerto Banús is the main departure point for luxury yacht charters, with its marina packed with superyachts and exclusive boutiques. The smaller Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo de Marbella) offers a more laid-back atmosphere and is close to the old town. Cabopino Harbour, a few miles east, gives access to the sand dunes of Artola. Gibraltar can be seen on clear days from the Strait, and the nearby town of Estepona is a popular lunch stop. The Bay of Málaga provides sheltered cruising grounds, and the island of Alborán lies further offshore but is a challenge for day trips. You can browse boats for your dates in a few minutes.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Marbella?

Prices for private boat hire in Marbella vary by boat type, duration and season, but you can expect to pay from €350 to €3,000 per day. A half-day rental of a small motorboat (up to 6 guests) starts around €350, while a full-day catamaran for up to 12 guests costs from €1,200. Crewed yachts with a skipper and host can reach €2,500 or more per day. It is advisable to browse boats for your dates on BoatHire24 to compare prices and availability across different craft. One option worth a look is this motor yacht.

Which boat to rent?

  1. Motorboat (e.g. Azimut 58): Ideal for fast cruising along the coast, with capacity for 10-12 guests, from €1,500 per day. You can view this motor yacht on BoatHire24 to see its deck plan and photos.
  2. Catamaran (e.g. Lagoon 52): Perfect for stable family days, sleeping up to 8 overnight or 12 day trips, from €1,200 per half-day.
  3. RIB or day cruiser: Great for watersports and active groups, carrying up to 8 guests, from €400 for 4 hours.
  4. Luxury yacht (e.g. Sunseeker 86): A premium option for entertaining, with full crew and up to 12 guests, from €2,500 per day.

Cost breakdown for private boat hire in Marbella

  • Shared tour (per person on a catamaran): €60-120
  • Small motorboat half-day (private, up to 6 guests): €350-600
  • Catamaran full-day (private, up to 12 guests): €1,200-2,000
  • Crewed yacht full-day (skipper and host, 12 guests): €2,000-3,500

When to go and do you need a licence?

The best season for boat hire in Marbella runs from May to October, when temperatures are warm and the Poniente wind keeps the sea calm. July and August are peak months, with prices up to 30% higher. For boating in Spain, a recreational licence is required for vessels over a certain power and length, but bareboat skippers must hold a valid qualification (e.g. PPI or ICC). The operator on BoatHire24 will confirm exact requirements for each boat. You can also see more at browse the listings.

If you are a confident sailor, the Marina La Bajadilla in Marbella old town offers cheaper mooring and is a short walk from the best tapas bars.

At a glance

  • Price band per day: €350 to €3,500
  • Peak months: July, August
  • Trip length: half-day (4 hours) or full-day (8 hours)
  • Maximum capacity: up to 12 guests on most private charters
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Marbella

Can I rent a boat in Marbella without a licence?

Yes, you can charter a crewed boat with a skipper, which is the most common arrangement for private hire in Marbella.

What is the best area for snorkelling near Marbella?

The protected bay of Calahonda and the rocky coves around Cabopino offer clear water and a chance to see marine life.

How far is Gibraltar from Marbella by boat?

Gibraltar is approximately 45 nautical miles south of Marbella, a 1.5-hour trip in a fast motorboat.

Can I anchor for lunch at Estepona?

Yes, the bay of Estepona offers a safe anchorage with calm waters and several waterfront restaurants accessible by dinghy.

Whether you are visiting for a weekend or a month, hiring a private boat in Marbella gives you the freedom to explore the Costa del Sol on your own terms. To browse the listings and compare options from local owners, visit BoatHire24. If you own a vessel in Marbella, you can list your boat on the marketplace to earn extra income during the busy 2026 season.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Marbella and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
